mvp_scope.mvp_goal: Let a business aggregate supplier products and services, organize them in a unified asset library, calculate costs and margins, build simple bundles or production recipes, and generate one customer-ready sales output.
mvp_scope.mvp_boundary: Prioritize reliable supplier source setup, file/manual import for products and services, unified asset organization, core pricing and margin calculations, simple bundles or recipes, and one quote/catalog/price-book output path before broad automation, AI, analytics, portals, enterprise, marketplace, procurement, or deep integrations.

deferred_features.name: Broad automatic supplier portal synchronization
deferred_features.reason: Integration variability, credential handling, and supplier terms create high early complexity.

deferred_features.name: Customer portals and secure customer login
deferred_features.reason: Customer access control should follow validation of the internal quote, catalog, and price book workflows.

deferred_features.name: Advanced AI assistants
deferred_features.reason: AI search, pricing suggestions, catalog generation, and quote drafting require validated data quality, retrieval, moderation, and usage controls.

deferred_features.name: Advanced analytics dashboards
deferred_features.reason: Reporting value depends on enough real supplier, quote, catalog, and margin activity.

deferred_features.name: Ecommerce, CRM, accounting, and storage integrations
deferred_features.reason: Integration priorities should follow customer validation.

deferred_features.name: Enterprise governance
deferred_features.reason: SSO, MFA, audit logging, retention, and compliance reporting should be staged after core product-market validation unless an enterprise launch buyer requires them.

deferred_features.name: Procurement, RFQs, bidding, and marketplace
deferred_features.reason: Marketplace and procurement workflows are separate operating models from internal sourcing and quoting.

deferred_features.name: Production planning, capacity management, white label, and global expansion
deferred_features.reason: These are later-stage operating systems that depend on validated core adoption.

launch_critical_systems: Product and service import or manual entry

phase_1_foundation: Unified product, service, bundle, and recipe data model
phase_4_sales_output: Quote builder or first validated output type

optimization_recommendations.recommendation: Pick one initial vertical and model its most common product plus service bundle workflow.
Ship file/manual import and import review before scheduled synchronization or portal automation.
Choose one launch-critical output type, likely quote or price book, before building every catalog, proposal, portal, and publishing mode.
Treat AI and integrations as acceleration layers after the core data model, pricing logic, and output workflows are validated.

complexity_reductions.preserved_value: Users can still centralize supplier data and build priced offerings through controlled imports.
Users can still generate and share customer-ready outputs.
The product remains focused on internal sourcing, pricing, bundling, and quoting.
